briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 3, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis, Threatening Daily Life and Natural Ecosystems
sections.content: Domestically, record-breaking diurnal temperature ranges are occurring, threatening daily life to the extent that the perception of 'spring disappearing' is spreading [1]. Internationally, the crisis situation due to climate change is deepening in the Lake Chad Basin and the Sahel region, and rapid temperature fluctuations in spring are also worsening [20, 21]. Notably, Europe has been named 'the fastest warming continent' in a recent report, and analyses suggest that recent heatwave phenomena are a result of anthropogenic climate change [28, 24]. Concerns are also being raised that deep-sea corals may be more vulnerable to climate change, along with warnings that prophetic visions of urban overheating and climate change are becoming a reality [23, 26].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Implementation
sections.content: Domestically, educational and experiential programs for carbon neutrality implementation are actively operating, encouraging citizen participation [3, 5, 8, 10]. Jeju Carbon Zero-nia attracted 3,400 visitors in its opening year, serving as a hub for climate crisis experiential education [3, 11]. Daejeon Daedeok-gu and Siheung Eco Center are also operating carbon neutrality experiential programs [5, 8, 10]. The used-item trading platform 'Danggeun' (Carrot) is collaborating with government and public institutions to present used-item trading as a climate action, contributing to the spread of carbon neutrality [6]. These efforts emphasize the importance of practicing carbon neutrality in daily life and focus on encouraging voluntary participation from citizens.

sections.title: 🌱 Trends in Renewable Energy Transition and Technological Innovation
sections.content: Domestic and international efforts for technological innovation and infrastructure development for energy transition are continuing. Domestically, scientific advancements are being made to maximize carbon-neutral technologies, such as KAIST identifying electric double-layer molecular structure variations and uncovering the 'key secret' of energy reactions [7, 4]. Korea Midland Power (KOMIPO) has launched its 'Renewable Energy Operations Headquarters' and is enhancing AI-based integrated operations, thereby advancing its renewable energy systems [13, 16]. Furthermore, interest in companies responding to renewable energy is growing in manufacturing sectors facing increasing Middle East risks [14]. Internationally, the deployment of renewable energy is expanding, with Australia's solar power generation setting a new record high in the first quarter of 2026 [17]. Provincial council member Lim Chang-hwi called for the active promotion of the 'Renewable Energy Citizen Participation Fund,' demonstrating a policy commitment to the spread of renewable energy [15].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Climate Policy and Raising Social Awareness
sections.content: Social awareness and policy discussions regarding the climate crisis are also actively continuing. The importance of the climate agenda is being emphasized in upcoming elections, with editorials being published advocating for the election of representatives who will address the climate crisis threatening daily life [2, 18]. Meanwhile, movements to strengthen national-level climate crisis response capabilities have been observed, such as the appointment of Shin Kang-min as the Director-General for Planning and Coordination at the Secretariat of the National Climate Crisis Response Committee within the Office for Government Policy Coordination [19]. Candidate Park Chan-dae announced a pledge to provide Eeum Card points for practicing carbon neutrality, a policy proposal aimed at encouraging voluntary climate action from citizens [9]. This demonstrates that responding to the climate crisis is emerging as a major agenda across politics, economy, and society, beyond being merely an environmental issue.

briefing.closing: Based on the severity of the climate crisis discussed in today's briefing and the various domestic and international efforts to address it, we once again emphasize the importance of continuous attention and action from all of us. This concludes today's climate briefing.
